---
title: "Exploratory Data Analysis — Gapminder Dataset"
description: "Analysing global development trends using Python, pandas and Plotly."
author: "Meghana"
date: "2025-01-15"
image: ../../images/project1.png
categories: [Python, EDA, Plotly, Data Visualisation]
---

## Overview

In this project I explored the Gapminder dataset to understand relationships 
between GDP, life expectancy, and population across countries and years.

![Global development trends visualised using the Gapminder dataset](../../images/project1.png){fig-alt="Gapminder scatter plot" width="80%"}

## Libraries Used

| Library | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `pandas` | Data manipulation |
| `plotly.express` | Interactive visualisations |

## Analysis

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Load built-in Gapminder dataset
df = px.data.gapminder()

# Filter for most recent year
df_2007 = df[df['year'] == 2007]

# Interactive scatter plot
fig = px.scatter(
    df_2007,
    x="gdpPercap",
    y="lifeExp",
    size="pop",
    color="continent",
    hover_name="country",
    log_x=True,
    title="GDP per Capita vs Life Expectancy (2007)",
    labels={"gdpPercap": "GDP per Capita", "lifeExp": "Life Expectancy"}
)
fig.show()
```

## Key Findings

::: {.callout-note}
Countries with higher GDP per capita generally show higher life expectancy, 
though with diminishing returns at very high income levels.
:::

- **Europe and Oceania** consistently show the highest life expectancy.
- **Africa** shows the most variation, suggesting other factors at play.
